title: Crunchy Nostalgic Turkish Blackberries Bake
description: A light, nostalgic Turkish dessert that blends the sweetness of blackberries with a crisp oat topping and a subtle almond flour base.
instructions: Line an 8×8‑inch baking dish with parchment paper or lightly grease it.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the almond flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate bowl, beat the eggs, honey, vanilla extract, and lemon zest until well combined.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and stir just until combined.
Fold in the unsweetened applesauce until the batter is smooth.
Gently fold in the blackberries, reserving a few for topping if desired.
Pour the batter into the prepared dish, spreading it evenly.
Sprinkle the puffed rice cereal evenly over the batter, pressing lightly to secure.
Bake for 20–25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
Serve warm or at room temperature, optionally dusted with a pinch of powdered sugar or a sprinkle of lemon zest.
